SaSMaster
Themes Tokens Chart Types Report Catalog Characters Motion
v8 · 209 Catalog Entries
SaSMaster · Bloomberg Terminal for Media

Canonical Brand
Design System

Five locked themes, universal tokens, chart language, and reporting catalog — the single source of truth for every artifact, portal screen, and visualization. v8 expands the catalog to 209 entries across 7 families, adding 93 new entries: Rights & Royalties (RR-001–020), Cross Platform (CP-001–012, CP-ADV-001–010), Social Analytics (SA-001–020), Digital Analytics (DA-001–014), and Other / Media Intelligence (OT-001–008) anchored by the Evan Shapiro / ESHAP framework. Chart type library grows from 20 to 46 types.

Sub-Styles

v7 · LIVE
01

Five Locked Themes

Five locked themes — each a complete visual language with full background and accent palettes, typography, and motion. Use exactly as specified. No mixing of font families across themes.

DARK — Cinematic Terminal
Bloomberg meets cinema · Default portal skin · All five portal verticals

THEME 05 · Creative / RoughJS 05
HANDSKETCH ANIMATION — Three Sub-Styles
RoughJS rendering engine · Caveat / Raleway / Patrick Hand
Each sub-style has its own background, font, roughness, fill pattern, tooltip, and texture. Sub-styles are not interchangeable within a single report.
Analyst's Notebook
05-A · Analyst's Notebook
Font: Caveat (cursive, bold)
BG: Warm cream + ruled lines + CSS grain
Charts: Hachure fill · roughness 1.8
Nav: Red margin underline (#c0392b)
Tooltip: Sticky note, rotated
Accent: #e05555 · green #1D9E75
Texture: CSS grain over ruled lines
Audience: Analysts, researchers, internal intel
Blueprint Draft
05-B · Blueprint Draft
Font: Raleway 200 (ultra-light)
BG: Deep navy #253c6e + cyan grid
Charts: Zigzag fill · roughness 0.8 (precise)
Nav: Cyan panel underline, all-caps, spaced
Tooltip: Dark navy engineering panel
Accent: rgba(80,255,160,0.8)
Texture: Edge vignette, corner darkening
Audience: Engineers, architects, tech reviews
Studio Sketchbook
05-C · Studio Sketchbook
Font: Patrick Hand (casual cursive)
BG: Warm white + canvas grain + radial glow
Charts: Cross-hatch fill · roughness 2.2 (most expressive)
Nav: Terracotta underline
Tooltip: Ink card, bold border + drop shadow
Accent: Studio accent · green #4a8c6f
Texture: Vertical canvas grain
Audience: Client pitches, creatives, marketing
02

Universal Design Tokens — Dark System (Theme 01) Reference

This document renders in Theme 02 (Light) — the swatches below are reference values for the dark portal build. Apply verbatim — never hardcode hex colors inline.

DARK PORTAL TOKEN REFERENCE — values as they appear in the dark portal, shown here for reference only

--bg-base#0a0a0f

--orange (rare)#f97316
Text · Border · Layout

--text-secondary#94a3b8

Portal nav order is fixed and non-negotiable. CONTENT is always first. Active state = sky blue underline (dark theme: lavender underline). Dr. Scoop FAB always bottom-right on every portal screen.
03

Chart Type Library & Visual Language

The canonical chart vocabulary — 20 types — verified against production source code (RSG Scoop / ra-linear-analytics) and the Advertising reference stack. Covers both the generic encoding-pattern primitives (column, line, area, scatter, stacked, grouped, spline, venn, combo, gauge, pie/donut, data grid, treemap, radar) and the SaSMaster domain-specific variants (Day-Part Heatmap, Sankey Flow, Quintile Power Curve, Diverging M/F Skew, RoughJS hand-drawn). Each type has a locked color order, transparent background rule, and grid-line spec, and is rendered in Highcharts unless otherwise noted. This inventory is the single source of truth for which engine renders what.

Production Charting & Grid Stack

SOURCE-VERIFIED
LibraryVersionRoleWhere
Highcharts11.4.8Primary charting engine — nearly every chart (13 modules: drilldown, boost, sankey, venn, treemap, streamgraph, solid-gauge, series-label…)Scoop / ra-linear-analytics · RAHighChart.jsx
↳ highcharts-react-official3.2.1React binding—
↳ highcharts-custom-events3.0.10Click / drill interactions—
dimple.js2.3.0Bubble / Performance Scatter ONLY (D3-based)Scoop · BubbleChart
D3 + topojson + chroma4.13.0DMA choropleth map, color scales, easing (d3-ease)Scoop · DMAMap.jsx
ParamQuery Grid (PQGrid)9.0.0ALL data grids / tables (telecast, PID, metadata, forecast)Scoop · RAPQGrid.jsx
react-circular-progressbar—Dashboard drilldown gaugeScoop · DrilldownGuage.jsx
Tableau.twbxAdvertising heritage analytics — Competitive, Pricing, Inventory, Revenue dashboards (rebuilt in Highcharts for product)AD-IQ lineage
Excel / PowerPoint—Ad-sales finance & yield reporting; Plan-it / AD-IQ module specsAD-IQ lineage
Not production (do not specify): amCharts (v4 + v5 — abandoned, lives only in _AM/_BKP files), AG Grid (demo only — PQGrid is canonical), Chart.js (never used in Scoop; only in retired audience-scoop prototypes).
Dark Theme Chart Color Order (Theme 01)
1st: lavender #a78bfa
2nd: teal #22d3ee
3rd: gold #f59e0b
4th: green #10b981
5th: red #ef4444
Grid lines: rgba(255,255,255,0.04) · Axis labels: Share Tech Mono + --text-secondary · BG: always transparent
Bar Chart
All Themes
Rating / reach comparisons. Grouped or stacked. Never solid BG on chart area.
Universal
Bubble / Scatter
Dark · Narrative
Network positioning, audience concentration, ROI vs budget. D3 force-directed.
Theme 01 Theme 04
Dual-Axis Line + Bar
Media Mantra
Ratings AA (bars: orange) + HUT/PUT (line: blue). Scoop multi-network dashboard.
Theme 03
Day-Part Heatmap
Media Mantra · Narrative
7×7 day-by-daypart grid. Index 143 = Thu 12PM peak. Traffic-light color scale.
Theme 03 Theme 04
Sankey / Flow
Dark · Narrative
Content flow, rights territory, audience migration. Dotted-ribbon animated style. SVG flex:1.
Theme 01 Theme 04
Quintile Power Curve
All Themes
Viewing concentration. Q1=67% of all viewing avg 26 titles. Quintile markers at each decile.
Universal
Diverging Bar — M/F Skew
Dark · Light
Gender/demo skew. Mirrored from center axis. Lavender (Female) / Teal (Male) in dark; purple/blue in light.
Theme 01 Theme 02
RoughJS Hand-Drawn
Handsketch Theme 05
Hachure / zigzag / cross-hatch fill. Roughness 0.8–2.2. Re-animates on theme switch.
Theme 05-A/B/C
Line
All Themes
Time series & trends. Path continuity shows progression. Multi-series capable.
Universal
Area
All Themes
Cumulative change over time. Fill shows composition. Often stacked.
Universal
Stacked Bar
All Themes
Part-to-whole composition. Segments stack to 100% or total.
Universal
Spline
All Themes
Smoothed line interpolation. Softens discrete points, suggests continuity.
Universal
Grouped Bar
All Themes
Multiple series side-by-side. Direct comparison within categories.
Universal
Venn
All Themes
Set overlap & exclusive membership. Area proportional to count.
Universal
Combo
All Themes
Column + Line dual-axis. Mix chart types to show relationships.
Universal
Gauge
All Themes
Single value as filled arc. Target/benchmark indicators. KPI display.
Universal
Pie / Donut
All Themes
Part-to-whole at a moment. Arc length = proportion. 3–5 slices optimal.
Universal
Data Grid
All Themes
Tabular records with sort/filter. PQGrid rendering. Metadata tables.
Universal
Treemap
All Themes
Hierarchical rectangle subdivision. Parent-child nesting by area.
Universal
Radar
All Themes
Multi-axis radial comparison. Profile/capability scoring.
Universal

26 New Chart Types — v8 Expansion

CT-NEW-01→22 · CT-ADV-01→04 Cross Platform · CP Advertising · Rights & Royalties · Digital Analytics · Social Analytics
Donut Badge Grid
Cross Platform
Grid of partial-fill donut rings per demo, each with signed delta badge. Weekly reach % by platform × demographic.
CT-NEW-01
Outside Currency Card
Cross Platform
Side-panel KPI callout with show image/logo thumbnail and single hero metric. Pull-quote companion to data tables.
CT-NEW-02
3-Circle Audience Overlap Venn
Cross Platform
Three overlapping circles (Live / DVR / OTT-VOD) with % labels in each exclusive and overlap segment for deduplication.
CT-NEW-03
Index Ranking Table + Highlight
Cross Platform
Programs × platforms index table. Highest value per column conditionally highlighted in amber. Performance vs platform average.
CT-NEW-04
Paired Expansion Bar
Cross Platform
L+7 audience vs 30-day complete run per program. Shows audience growth from extended OTT/VOD measurement window.
CT-NEW-05
Multi-Period Pacing Table
Advertising
Quarter-banded pacing table with Estimate / Actual / % Diff sub-columns. Live vs C3 dual-stream rows.
CT-ADV-01
4-Group CP Network Table
Cross Platform · Advertising
Ranked network table with 4 grouped column sets (Linear / Digital / Social / XPlatform). Ranked by XPlatform Total. Amber = highest per column.
CT-ADV-02
Paired Estimate vs Actual Scorecard
Cross Platform · Advertising
2-panel layout (Estimates / Actuals), each with 4-row platform breakdown. Campaign TRP delivery reconciliation format.
CT-ADV-03
Reach Curve Multi-Panel
Advertising
Four small-multiple S-curve charts (Linear / Digital / Social / XPlatform). % Reach on Y vs TRP accumulation on X per panel.
CT-ADV-04
Stacked GIVT Component Bar
Advertising
Time-series bar divided into 4 fraud-type segments (Spider/Bot, Data Center, Invalid Browser, High Frequency). Summary table above chart.
CT-NEW-06
Rights Gantt Chart
Rights & Royalties
Each title = one row. Sold windows color-coded by licensee; available windows always green; dashed border = placeholder. Interactive drag-and-drop.
CT-NEW-07
Page Flow / Sankey Navigation
Digital Analytics
Left-to-right directional tree from a selected page. Line widths proportional to traffic share. 3 levels deep. Forward or backward variants.
CT-NEW-08
Smooth Line Video Engagement
Digital Analytics
Non-stepped line: X=video time segments, Y=% views for that segment. Peaks indicate replay/rewind. Distinct from standard trend line.
CT-NEW-09
Dual-Axis Min-by-Min Social × Ratings
Social Analytics
Social PPM filled area + Nielsen minute audience dashed line on shared X clock axis. Event annotation pins. SaSMaster signature report.
CT-NEW-10
Social Competitive Radar
Social Analytics
Spider/radar with social KPI axes (Followers, Engagement Rate, Posting Frequency, SOV, Growth Rate). Each competitor = separate polygon.
CT-NEW-11
Social Buzz Spike Area + Annotation
Social Analytics
Filled area (social volume over time) with vertical event annotation pins (episode air, trailer drop, controversy). Baseline reference line.
CT-NEW-12
Twitter Interaction Distribution
Social Analytics
Media-specific Twitter taxonomy donut: Embedded Media Clicks / Detail Expands / URL Clicks / Hashtag Clicks / Retweets / Replies / Favorites.
CT-NEW-13 · RSG IP
Paired Delta Badge Row
Social Analytics
KPI cards with absolute value + colored arrow + % vs prior period. Two-tier: Followers / Impressions / Interactions → Replies / Retweets / Favorites.
CT-NEW-14 · RSG IP
Emoji Reaction Distribution
Social Analytics
Donut showing Facebook emoji reactions (Like / Love / Haha / Wow / Sorry / Angry) as % of total. Reveals emotional tenor beyond raw engagement.
CT-NEW-15
3-Metric Bubble Chart
Digital Analytics
X=Metric A, Y=Metric B, bubble SIZE=Metric C. Three-dimensional relationship. Upper-right large bubbles = clear winners.
CT-NEW-16
Paired KPI Delta Cards
Cross Platform · Advertising
Two colored cards showing large signed % for Q/Q and Y/Y comparison. Standard for any trend metric benchmark.
CT-NEW-17
OTT Device Bubble w/ Logos
Cross Platform
Proportional filled circles per device tier (Smart TV / Mobile / Gaming) showing OTT audience share %. Brand logos anchor each tier.
CT-NEW-18
Hexagonal Visit-Length Distribution
Cross Platform
Four hex cells showing % by visit-length band (0–10 / 10–30 / 30–60 / 60+ min). Annotation callout on longest-duration cell.
CT-NEW-19
Challenge / Alternative Matrix
Cross Platform · Advertising
2-row structured table: row 1 = challenge domain, row 2 = available alternative methodology. Qualitative OTT measurement gap framework.
CT-NEW-20
Royalty Posting Drilldown Bar
Rights & Royalties
Stacked bar of royalties posted per licensor / period broken by deal attributes (territory, rights type, base vs. overage).
CT-NEW-21
Lifestyle Donut w/ Icon Center
Cross Platform
Donut with thematic icon center. 3-segment survey-response distribution. Used in demographic lifestyle profiling.
CT-NEW-22
04

Universal Reporting Catalog — 209 Entries

Seven families: PROGRAMMING · MARKETING · ADVERTISING · RIGHTS & ROYALTIES · CROSS PLATFORM · SOCIAL ANALYTICS · DIGITAL ANALYTICS. Each entry is locked with verified chart type, render library, and theme assignment. Source-verified catalog powers the BLESS → DEPOSIT → PROMOTE pipeline. v8 adds 93 new entries across 6 new families: Rights & Royalties (RR-001–020), Cross Platform (CP-001–022), Social Analytics (SA-001–020), Digital Analytics (DA-001–014), and Other / Media Intelligence (OT-001–008) anchored by the Evan Shapiro / Media Cartographer framework.

209 SOURCE-VERIFIED 9 PENDING (Marketing source audit) Libraries: Highcharts dimple.js D3 PQGrid Tableau
PROGRAMMING Family · 75 (P01–P75) VERIFIED — Scoop / RSG Audience / Audience Science / Nielsen / BET Advisory / nPower / MediaSpectrum / AdvantEdge / ANE WARP

Multi-Network Dashboard
Programming
Network tiles → drilldown column + circular gauge. QTD AA by network.

Highcharts

Programming

Horizontal Bar

Program rating trajectory over time. Multi-series line.
Line
Highcharts

Programming
Genre share + trend. Combination column (share) + line (trend).

Column
Highcharts

Venn

P10

Territory × genre licensing opportunity heatmap. $279B market lens.
HeatmapMultinet
Highcharts
P11
Box Office Intelligence
Programming
Theatrical performance: budget vs gross, ROI scatter, studio ranking. Budget/gross correlation.
Scatter + BarThe Numbers
dimple.js
P12
Audience Flow
Programming
Lapsed · New · Retained viewer migration with Super-PID overlay. Where did viewers come from and go. Sankey / alluvial flow.
Sankey / AlluvialScoop · One-Pager
D3
P13
Smart Grid
Programming
Daypart × Day dynamic heatmap across all research metrics. RTG benchmark + competitive overlay, drill-down to telecast.
Heatmap GridRSG Smart Grid
Highcharts
P14
Audience Profile
Programming
Telecast / Series / Network 360: demo profile, reach, retention, premiere-vs-repeat split. Per-title lifecycle snapshot.
Combo Small-MultipleScoop · One-Pager
Highcharts
P15
DMA / Local Market
Programming
Local-market viewership by DMA. US choropleth + rank. Pending PID-DMA mapping for respondent-level local cuts.
Choropleth MapNielsen Local
Highcharts Maps
P16
Placement & Coverage Forecast
Programming
Predicted Rtg% vs network Rtg% across candidate networks. Box-whisker delta bands. Powers Scoop Suggestions placement recs.
Box Plot + ForecastProphet · SCOOP
Highcharts
P17
%GSHR/%SCH Affinity Index
Programming
Network / program affinity for a target audience, indexed to a 100 baseline. Efficiency of viewing vs schedule supply.
Index Bar (100-base)Audience Science
Highcharts
P18
Affinity Program Ranker
Programming
Dual-population ranker (all vs heavy-segment), heat-shaded, with %GSHR/%Sch index column. Decodes what core viewers watch.
Heat-Shaded GridScoop Ranker
PQGrid
P19
Reach × Time-Spent Quad
Programming
Genre / subgenre efficiency: Avg Reach% vs Avg Time-Spent Viewing. Bubble size = Share of GRP. Quadrant positioning.
Bubble QuadScoop
dimple.js
P20
Perceptual Map
Programming
Competitive landscape: all-nets / genres Avg Audience against M/F skew, median age, and HoH race. Demo positioning.
Bubble QuadOne-Pager · Scoop
dimple.js
P21
Viewer Segmentation Matrix
Programming
Gold / Occasional / Surfer / Lite frequency × duration segmentation. Composition, reach, minutes-per-day, viewing-day share.
Freq×Dur MatrixMTV Insights
Highcharts
P22
Segment / Persona Profile
Programming
Segment KPI donuts (%audience, %minutes, skew, median age, reach) plus competitive program & network context tables.
KPI Donuts + GridMTV Insights
Highcharts + React
P23
Supply vs Demand
Programming
Share of GRP vs Share of Duration, paired bars. Surfaces over/under-scheduled genres — viewing demand against schedule supply.
Paired BarScoop · Gracenote
Highcharts
P24
Studio / Distributor Share
Programming
Studio / distributor share of viewing & duration across network groups. Rights and content-licensing lens.
Paired BarScoop · The Numbers
Highcharts
P25
Tabular Reports
Programming
Telecast · New/Lapsed PID · Metadata · Movie & TV Tracker · Forecast Audit. Generic Top-N data grid.
Data Grid
PQGrid
P26
Cable Ecosystem Health
Programming
Universe estimate (UE), PUT/TUT, ICD/SVOD share trends by demo over time. Multi-series line showing cable universe erosion vs streaming growth. Cord-cutter overlay.
Multi-Line + AreaNielsen NPower
Highcharts
P27
Cord-Cutter / Cord-Never Profile
Programming
Demographic, territory, county-type, SVOD ownership and MVPD provider loss profile for cord-cutters vs retained cable. Audience vulnerability lens.
Bar + Donut ProfileNielsen Techedge
Highcharts
P28
Network × Daypart Rank Grid
Programming
Hour × DoW heatmap of network rank and average impressions. Color-coded green (best) to red (worst). Best/worst timeslot identification at granular hour-slot level.
Heat-Shaded Rank GridNielsen NPower
PQGrid
P29
Black Share Gainers / Losers
Programming
QoQ and YoY network share delta ranked by HOH Black P18-49 gain/loss. Annotated with content-shift driver. Horizontal waterfall + ranked bar.
Waterfall + Ranked BarNielsen Techedge
Highcharts
P30
Top Viewer Segment Analysis
Programming
Heavy / Gold / Occasional / Lite viewer composition: weighted tuning minutes, % of total viewing, YoY change. Multi-network comparison across competitive set.
Stacked Bar + TrendNielsen NPM
Highcharts
P31
Viewer Duplication Matrix (Program)
Programming
Program-level audience overlap: % of Program A viewers who also watch Program B. Heat-shaded cross-tab grid. Scheduling adjacency and acquisition intelligence.
Heat-Shaded Cross-TabNielsen NPM
PQGrid
P32
Top Viewer Device & Provider Profile
Programming
Top-viewer penetration for Smart TV, SVOD, OTT device type, and MVPD provider. Bar + index gauge vs universe average. Tracks audience technology migration risk.
Bar + Index GaugeNielsen NPM
Highcharts
P33
Content Avails Tracker
Programming
Quarterly movie vs TV series avails volume over time. Stacked area showing inventory depth by type and genre. Linked to scheduling opportunity gap vs demand.
Area (Stacked)Rights & Scheduling Systems
Highcharts
P34
Content Spend Analysis
Programming
Content investment by Original vs Acquired, Movie vs Series, and scripted sub-types. Pie + treemap with average annual spend by category. Budget allocation intelligence.
Pie + TreemapFinancial Planning Actuals
Highcharts
P35
Asset Allocation Optimizer
Programming
Optimal Movie vs TV Series hours by daypart × DoW. Binary optimization model (AdVant™) output — current vs recommended allocation grid with predicted share lift delta.
Allocation Grid + Variance BarAdVant™ Optimizer
Highcharts + React
P36
What-If Scenario Simulator
Programming
Interactive strategy builder: select scheduling option (strip / movies-first / mixed / daily-focus) → live predicted share vs baseline. Evaluates Options A/B/C/D side-by-side.
Interactive ScenarioProphet™ · AdVant™
Highcharts + React
P37
ML Feature Importance
Programming
CatBoost / LightGBM feature importance scores by individual feature and category (Temporal Cyclical, Content Share, Viewing Pattern, Seasonal). Grouped horizontal bar.
Grouped Horizontal BarProphet™ CatBoost
Highcharts
P38
Content Acquisition Recommendations
Programming
Ranked acquisition target list: title, suggested time slot, confidence level, lapsed-PID affinity score, total weighted minutes watched. Grid with heat-coded confidence column.
Ranked Grid + Confidence HeatScoop · Prophet™
PQGrid
P39
Network Ratings Trend — Total Day
Programming
P18-49 AA% (LSD) across total day, by network. QoQ and YoY % change annotated. Coverage-area qualified. Multi-series trend with YoY delta line overlay.
Line + Dual-AxisMIT · Nielsen NPM
Highcharts
P40
Audience Retention & Lapse Analysis
Programming
Retained vs. Lapsed vs. New viewer split (millions + %) QoQ. 6-minute qualifier threshold. Donut showing cohort composition, waterfall showing net audience delta by quarter.
Donut + WaterfallAMRLD · Nielsen NPM
Highcharts
P41
Lapsed Audience Migration Map
Programming
Where lapsed PIDs went: top 10 networks and top 10 programs by weighted minutes absorbed. Ranked horizontal bar with YoY column. Sankey variant for flow visualization.
Ranked Bar + SankeyAMRLD · Scoop
Highcharts
P42
Audience Attribute & Profile Report
Programming
HoH Income, HoH Race SoV, Gender Skew, Median Age, device profile (iOS/Android/Broadcast-Only). Share of Viewing (SoV) indexed vs. universe. Diverging bars + radar profile.
Diverging Bar + RadarNielsen NPM · AMRLD
Highcharts
P43
Top Viewer Concentration Analysis
Programming
Top-N viewer cohort as % of total network viewing (Weighted Minutes/Week). Multi-network side-by-side. YoY change in concentration. Stacked bar split: Top Viewers vs. All Others.
Stacked Bar + KPI CardsNielsen NPM
Highcharts
P44
Multi-Network Competitive Ratings Trend
Programming
Network vs. network Black P18-49 ratings trend across 24+ months. YoY % change annotated per network. PUT universe trend as reference baseline. Grouped multi-series line.
Grouped Multi-LineNielsen NPM · NTI/NHI
Highcharts
P45
Panel Cohort Tracker
Programming
Tracks a defined PID cohort (e.g. Top 250 viewers) across two time periods: survivor count, migration to other networks, churn rate. Sankey flow + funnel showing panel attrition.
Sankey + FunnelAMRLD · NPM Panel
Highcharts
P46
ML Feature Importance — Ratings Drivers
Programming
GAM / XGBoost / Random Forest feature importance scores. Positive vs. negative impact split. Top-10 driver ranking per network. Diverging horizontal bar with importance magnitude axis.
Diverging Horizontal BarProphet™ · GAM · XGBoost
Highcharts
P47
Ratings Prediction & Timeslot Optimization
Programming
Forecast AA(000s) by daypart × day-of-week. Strongest and weakest timeslots for specific content types (movies, premieres, African American demos). Day-Part Heatmap + data grid.
Day-Part Heatmap + GridProphet™ · AMRLD
Highcharts + PQGrid
P48
Program Acquisition Scorecard
Programming
Content fit scoring table: series/movie name, suggested timeslot, confidence tier (High/Medium/Low), lapsed-PID total weighted minutes, affinity rationale. Heat-coded confidence column.
Data Grid + Confidence HeatScoop · AMRLD · Prophet™
PQGrid
P49
TV Series Genre Analysis — Audience Segment
Programming
Top genres ranked by GRPs across custom 12-month AMRLD audience segments (P2+, P18-49, P25-54). Top programs per genre per segment. Grouped column with segment-toggle filter.
Grouped Column + Ranked BarAMRLD · Scoop · Gracenote
Highcharts
P50
Cross-Network Viewing Index
Programming
BET (or target network) audience over-index on external network programs. %GSHR vs. %Schedule for candidate content. Affinity scoring via weighted minutes. Bubble scatter + indexed bar.
Bubble Scatter + Index BarAMRLD · Scoop · MIT
dimple.js
P51
Reach & Frequency — Program Schedule
Programming
Unduplicated HH/Person reach + frequency by program schedule. Progressive cume reach curve. Distinct from AD04 (advertiser campaign R&F) — this is content/programming schedule R&F.
Reach Curve + FrequencynPower · NPM
Highcharts
P52
Source & Destination Analysis
Programming
Where viewers came from before and where they went after a specific program minute. Flow-based audience path analysis. Distinct from P41 (quarterly cohort lapse) — this is minute-level viewing event tracking.
Flow / SankeynPower AMRLD
D3
P53
Length of Tune / Length of View
Programming
Average duration of tuning events per daypart (LOT) and average length of viewing per program episode (LOV). Measures program strength and appeal; assesses impact of format changes on audience hold.
Bar + DistributionnPower · MediaSpectrum
Highcharts
P54
NTile / Quad Viewer Segmentation
Programming
Divides viewers into N equal groups by viewing frequency × duration. Heavy/Medium/Light/Non-viewer quartile breakdown. Distinct from P21 (Gold/Occasional/Surfer/Lite) — frequency-based NTile method from nPower respondent level.
Freq×Dur NTile MatrixnPower · AMRLD
Highcharts
P55
DVR / Time-Shifted Viewing Analysis
Programming
Live vs. Live+SD vs. Live+3 vs. Live+7 vs. Beyond7 viewing distribution by program. TSV lift %, DVR playback share, and C3 ACM commercial rating impact. Time-shifting behavior by demo and series type.
Multi-Line + Lift BarnPower · MIT · C3
Highcharts
P56
Co-Viewing Report
Programming
Pairs of viewers watching the same program on the same set simultaneously. Household composition and co-viewing index by demo. Used to understand family/shared viewing dynamics for content and advertising.
Bar + ProfilenPower Co-View
Highcharts
P57
Program Persistence & Inheritance
Programming
Episode-to-episode audience carry-over (persistence/retention) and inherited audience from lead-in program. Measures programming stack efficiency, lead-in contribution, and loyal vs. transient viewer split.
Line + WaterfallAdvantEdge · nPower
Highcharts
P58
Audience Migration & Switching
Programming
Where viewers switched to/from at a program boundary — time-slot winner identification and tuning migration map. Distinct from P41 (quarterly cohort lapse) — this is real-time minute-boundary switching from AdvantEdge.
Sankey + Ranked BarAdvantEdge · nPower
Highcharts
P59
Rating Grid & Daily Recap
Programming
Hour-by-hour network ratings grid for any given day or week across all networks simultaneously. Multi-network, single-day AA(000) program flow. Comprehensive daily competitive view from MediaSpectrum.
Heat-Shaded GridMediaSpectrum · MIT
PQGrid
P60
Telecast Ranking
Programming
All telecasts ranked by impressions/ratings for a selected period. Program-level ranking across networks by demo. Distinct from P02 (Ranker Report) which is network/daypart executive bar — this is individual telecast level.
Ranked Data GridMediaSpectrum · nPower
PQGrid
P61
Daily Ranker
Programming
Programs ranked by impressions on a day-by-day basis within any selected week. Granular daily competitive ranking distinct from weekly or season averages. Surfaces day-specific winners and outliers.
Ranked Bar + GridMediaSpectrum · MIT
Highcharts
P62
Live & C3 Ranker
Programming
Side-by-side Live vs. C3 commercial ratings ranking across programs. Currency-grade ranking for ad sales. Shows which programs gain/lose the most from time-shifting on a commercial-minutes basis.
Dual Ranked BarMediaSpectrum · C3
Highcharts
P63
PAC Index & PAC Trend
Programming
Program Average of Commercials (PAC) indexed to Program Average. Answers what % of program viewers actually watched the commercials. PAC Trend shows week-by-week variation. Key retention-to-commercial metric.
Index Bar + Line TrendMediaSpectrum · C3
Highcharts
P64
Skip Report — Commercial Avoidance
Programming
Viewers who watched a program in its entirety vs. avoided a segment (promo, PSA, commercial break). AVO rate by daypart, pod, and program. Surfaces ad avoidance risk by content type and time placement.
Bar + Avoidance RateMediaSpectrum · AMRLD
Highcharts
P65
NTV / NHTV National + Hispanic Grid
Programming
Side-by-side National People Meter (NPM) and National Hispanic People Meter (NHPM) ratings for selected programs and networks. Dual-sample report surfaces Hispanic audience index vs. national average for content decisions.
Dual-Column GridMediaSpectrum · NTIH/NHIH
PQGrid
P66
MarketBreaks Demographic Report
Programming
Custom demographic cuts against program/network ratings: race (White/Black/Hispanic), HoH income, education, SVOD ownership, PC ownership, county size, territory. Sub-demographic indexing beyond standard age/sex cells via nPower Market Breaks.
Index Bar + GridnPower MarketBreaks
Highcharts
P67
Up-To-Date Daypart YoY Comparison
Programming
Daypart averages across multiple demo targets with automatic YoY comparison to comparable month, quarter, year, and broadcast season from the prior year. Network-level pacing view from MediaSpectrum Up-To-Date module.
Grouped Bar + YoY DeltaMediaSpectrum · MIT
Highcharts
P68
HMLN Report
Programming
3-NTile (Heavy/Medium/Light/Non-viewer) segmentation of prior 28-day network viewers applied to a premiere telecast. Shows premiere audience composition % by H/M/L/N segment. Frequency-based prior-viewing segmentation distinct from P21 duration-based segmentation.
Stacked Bar + Segment ProfileRSG Scoop · nPower NTile
Highcharts
P69
In-Episode Audience Retention & Churn
Programming
Minute-by-minute audience entry (Groups A1/A2/B/C/D by join time) and exit tracking within a single telecast. Metrics: total/avg minutes watched, % broadcast consumed, % who stayed to last 3 minutes, new + lost viewers, net viewers. Lead-in inheritance vs. fresh tune-in split.
Spline + Churn WaterfallRSG Scoop · nPower AMRLD
Highcharts
P70
Propensity Index Report
Programming
Top 100 cable + broadcast programs ranked by affinity index for Heavy and Medium viewers of a target series. NTile 1/2 CVG AA% indexed to composite. Diff-in-rank (Heavy/Medium vs. composite) color-coded +/−. Network font-coded: Broadcast = blue, target network = red.
Heat-Shaded Ranked GridRSG Scoop · nPower
PQGrid
P71
Road to Success — Premiere Forecast
Programming
Pre-premiere forecast model: HMLN segment composition × lead-in duplication % × scatter estimate → projected premiere audience by H/M/L/N. Combines NTile segmentation, R&F duplication, and scatter planning into a single premiere estimate output.
Forecast Bar + CompositionRSG Scoop · Prophet™
Highcharts
P72
Quarterly Cumulative Network Reach
Programming
13-week progressive cumulative reach by week for a network, broken out by Heavy/Medium/Light viewer segments and multi-demo (HH, P25-54, M25-54, P18-49, M18-49, M18-34, P2+). Shows cumulative audience build week-over-week across the full quarter.
Progressive Cume LineRSG Scoop · nPower
Highcharts
P73
Quarter-Hour Grid / Daypart Report
Programming
Per-network, per-quarter-hour program and AA(000) ratings for a selected night or daypart. Shows program flow across a broadcast night at 15-minute granularity with daypart summary totals. More granular than P59 (hour-level multi-network daily recap).
QH Chronological GridANE WARP · MIT
PQGrid
P74
Future Programming Guide
Programming
Forward-looking schedule grid (no ratings) showing planned programming by network and timeslot across a competitive set. FYI/scheduling data only — used for pre-air competitive planning before telecasts air. Possible combination into site report noted.
Schedule Grid (No Ratings)ANE WARP · FYI Data
PQGrid
P75
Program Search Report
Programming
Individual telecast ranker for a specific program searched by keyword/title. Shows AA(000), % change vs. ½hr lead-in, and % change vs. prior 4-week average by episode. Tracks series episode-level trending across a date range and competitive context.
Ranked Grid + % ChangeANE WARP · nPower
PQGrid
MARKETING Family · 14 (M01–M14) VERIFIED — C360 / Prophet / Multinet / PRIZM / RSG Advisory / ANE Networks / nPower
M01
Campaign Reach & Conversion
Marketing
On-air promo reach % and conversion by channel, daypart, Nielsen demo. Rolling campaign window.
Bar + LineC360 OnAir
M02
Post-Campaign Analysis
Marketing
Multi-platform performance: GRPs, reach, frequency by network group (On-Net / Off-Net / DMA / MVPD).
Grouped Bar + Data GridProphet 2.5
M03
Conversion Efficiency Scatter
Marketing
Reach vs. Conversion by channel — surfaces highest-ROI promotional outlets per dollar of inventory.
ScatterYounger Blitz
M04
Segment Affinity Matrix
Marketing
Network × Program affinity grid for custom proxy audience (PRIZM/MRI-seeded). Tile intensity = affinity index.
HeatmapMultinet Release 4.2B
M05
Multinet Plan vs. Actuals
Marketing
Planned vs. delivered GRPs and impressions across all networks. Campaign timeline with compliance view (Gantt).
Grouped Bar + GanttC360 Maps
M06
Psychographic Cluster Map
Marketing
Nielsen PRIZM Premier 68-segment audience profile. Lifestage Group × Social Group × affluence clustering.
Bubble ClusterPRIZM Premier
M07
Social Buzz Velocity
Marketing
Tweet volume × engagement rate × share of voice. 30-day rolling window.
Line + AreaX/Twitter
Highcharts
M08
Franchise Bubble Map
Marketing
Franchise vs competitor social SOV. Bubble size = audience reach. Force layout.
Bubble (Force)
D3
M09
Genre Arbitrage Report
Marketing
Audience appetite vs content supply gap. Scatter + shaded opportunity zone.
Scatter
dimple.js
M10
Promo Campaign Effectiveness Report
Marketing
On-air promo 1+/3+ reach by viewer segment (Heavy/Medium/Light/All), avg frequency, reach impressions, and dual-direction conversion (saw promo → watched show; watched show → saw promo). Segmented by campaign flight window (full campaign, pre-premiere, premiere week).
R&F + Conversion TablenPower · RSG Scoop
Highcharts
M11
Cross-Channel & Off-Channel Promo Analysis
Marketing
Per-network promo unit count, reach impressions, and conversion rates for cross-channel (sister networks) and off-channel (competitive) placements. Unduplicated reach + cross-channel lift % vs. on-channel baseline. Network-level ROI ranking for promo placement strategy.
Bar + Lift ComparisonnPower · RSG Scoop
Highcharts
M12
Pre/Post Launch Marketing KPI Dashboard
Marketing
Dual-phase campaign dashboard. Pre-Launch: OTX awareness/intent/network linkage, R&F pacing vs. goals, promo AVR, ePoll content interest, social engagement/sentiment/profile, digital visits by device. Post-Launch: live/L3/L7 ratings, digital viewership, new viewer %, audience migration, campaign reach/conversion by channel (on-air, cross-channel, off-air, social).
Multi-Section KPI DashboardOTX · iSpot · nPower · Listen First
Highcharts + React
M13
Daypart Tracker vs. Goals
Marketing
Network daypart AA(000) vs. stated ratings goals, broken out by Prime, Total Day, Sales Prime, and other custom dayparts. YoY % change at week, month, quarter, and full-year granularity. Pacing view used to assess whether network is on track vs. annual targets.
Grouped Bar + Goal LineANE WARP · MIT
Highcharts
M14
Season Report Card
Marketing
Full-season executive report: episode-by-episode ratings table, multi-season YoY comparison across all demos, time-shifting table (LSD/L3/C3/L7 lift %), AA audience composition (Afrcn/Non-Afrcn), audience profile (HoH Race, Income, Education, Territory), lead-out destination analysis (SOV %), top affinity programs (Heavy/Medium viewer ranker — cable + broadcast), repeat performance by daypart vs. network norms.
Multi-Section Report CardnPower · RSG Advisory · AMRLD
Highcharts + PQGrid
ADVERTISING Family · 27 (AD01–AD27) VERIFIED — AD-IQ / Plan-it / Tableau / Nielsen Monitor-Plus / Ad Intel / MediaSpectrum

AD-IQ: 10 product modules for the Viacom/Paramount Promo Platform. Plan-it: 2 analytical report surfaces. All mapped to Highcharts (heritage Tableau .twbx sources available).

AD01
POD Intelligence (Position, Audience, Retention)
Advertising
Position bar ranking + retention curve (line). Viewership drop-off by content daypart. Nielsen + Gracenote-mapped.
Position Bar + CurveAD-IQ Module
Highcharts
AD02
Network Zero Share Report
Advertising
Networks with zero spend by Category·ParentCo·Advertiser·Brand. Treemap + bar drill.
Bar / TreemapAD-IQ Module
Highcharts
AD03
Platform/Network Spend
Advertising
Spend hierarchy by group/cluster. Stacked bar + treemap drill by advertiser + brand.
Stacked Bar / TreemapAD-IQ Module
Highcharts
AD04
Campaign Reach & Frequency
Advertising
Reach curve (line) + frequency histogram. Plan-it "Reach vs Estimated", "Effective Reach 3+", "Reach by TRP/GRP".
Reach Curve + HistogramPlan-it
Highcharts
AD05
Target Discovery
Advertising
Bubble / cluster visualization of advanced target segments. Size/position encode affinity metrics.
Bubble / ClusterAD-IQ Module
dimple.js
AD06
Network Forecaster (CPM/GRPs)
Advertising
Forecast line + dual-axis (CPM vs. GRP predictions). Trend with confidence bands.
Forecast Line / Dual-AxisAD-IQ Module
Highcharts
AD07
Brand Awareness Tracking
Advertising
Tracking line + index gauge. Brand lift over campaign. Benchmark comparison.
Tracking Line / Index GaugeAD-IQ Module
Highcharts
AD08
Campaign ROI Index
Advertising
Scatter quadrant: ROI efficiency by channel. Surfaces highest-ROI outlets. Wendy's reference source.
Scatter QuadrantPlan-it / Wendy's
Highcharts
AD09
Campaign Simulator
Advertising
Interactive what-if scenario builder. Budget slider → live output projections.
Interactive ScenarioAD-IQ Module
Highcharts + React
AD10
Spend Radar / Market Share
Advertising
Multi-axis radial profile. Spend vs. market opportunity comparison (currently Tableau source).
Radar ChartPlan-it
Tableau
AD11
Actual vs. Estimated (Units/TRPs)
Advertising
Comparison bars: Plan vs. Actual units delivered across networks. Variance highlighting.
Comparison BarPlan-it
Highcharts
AD12
Seasonality Breakdown
Advertising
Area chart with variance bands. Q1–Q4 seasonal performance patterns. Rolling 52-week context.
Area + Variance BandsPlan-it
Highcharts
AD13
C3 Inventory & Network Yield
Advertising
Commercial ratings (C3), ad-inventory delivery, and revenue yield/min by network, daypart, and pod. Currency-grade inventory + yield tracking.
Bar + GridRSG Audience · One-Pager
Highcharts
AD14
CPM Efficiency Matrix
Advertising
CPM by network × demo × daypart vs rate card. Heatmap + rank table.
Heatmap + Table
HC + PQGrid
AD15
Share of Media Report
Advertising
Expenditures and share of a brand across all 17+ media types (Network TV, Cable, Syndication, Spot TV, Radio, Magazine, Newspaper, Internet, Outdoor, etc.). Full omni-media competitive spend allocation. Distinct from AD03 (platform/network only).
Stacked Bar + Share TableAd Intel · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD16
Share of Media vs. Share of Voice
Advertising
GRP delivery vs. dollar spend comparison by media type. Surfaces over- and under-delivery by channel — e.g., Cable receiving 47% of spend but delivering 78% of GRPs. Guides media mix reallocation decisions.
Paired Bar + Delivery IndexAd Intel · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD17
Category Share of Media
Advertising
All advertiser spend within a product category across all media types. Competitive set spend landscape by category (e.g., Restaurant-Quick Svc). Shows which media types the category prioritizes and total category investment level.
Treemap + Category BarAd Intel · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD18
Monthly Trend — Advertiser Spending
Advertising
Month-by-month spending trend for a brand or category over a 12-month period. Surfaces seasonal spend patterns, flight timing, heavier/lighter months, and YoY budget changes. Competitive brand comparison supported.
Multi-Line TrendAd Intel · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD19
Period Comparison — Competitive Spend
Advertising
YoY or period-over-period spend comparison by brand/category. Answers whether a brand is increasing or decreasing their advertising budget vs. prior period. Side-by-side bar with % change column.
Comparison Bar + % ChangeAd Intel · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD20
Flowchart / Campaign Flight View
Advertising
Weekly GRP delivery by flight, by brand, across networks. Visual representation of a competitive media plan over a broadcast year. Shows weekly GRP values in a calendar-grid format with cumulative totals per network and brand.
Gantt / Flight CalendarAd Intel · MediaSpectrum
Highcharts
AD21
Local Market Allocation — Spot TV
Advertising
National TV dollars allocated to each DMA based on % of TV households. Compares Network/Cable/Syndicated national spend vs. Spot TV local spend by market (e.g., Chicago, LA, New York). GRP delivery + dollar comparison by market.
DMA Bar + Allocation TableAd Intel · Spot TV
Highcharts
AD22
A-Position / FML POD Detail View
Advertising
First/Middle/Last (FML) POD placement detail report showing total units and dollars by pod position across national TV media types. Audience retention curve by pod position. Distinct from AD01 (POD Intelligence overview) — this is the FML summary report format.
Position Bar + Retention CurveMonitor-Plus · MediaSpectrum
Highcharts
AD23
Unit Length Trend
Advertising
Trend of commercial unit lengths (:10, :15, :30, :60) by network, category, and advertiser over time. Commercial format mix shift analysis. Shows industry migration from :30 standard to shorter or longer formats.
Stacked Area + TrendMediaSpectrum · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD24
Commercialization Report
Advertising
Commercial clutter by network, daypart, and category. Seconds of commercial time per hour, fill rate by pod, pod structure analysis. Shows how heavily a network commercializes its inventory vs. industry averages.
Bar + Clutter GridMediaSpectrum · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD25
Company / Copy Length Analysis
Advertising
Advertiser spend broken out by commercial creative length (:15 vs :30 vs :60). Creative execution efficiency lens — which length formats each advertiser or category uses most. Supports media buying and inventory pricing strategy.
Grouped Bar by DurationMediaSpectrum · Monitor-Plus
Highcharts
AD26
Competitive Spending Track
Advertising
Multi-year broadcast and cable network spend and total audience metrics by company. Multi-year competitive spend trajectory showing brand investment trends. Combines audience delivery with dollar spend for ROI context across competitors.
Multi-Line + Spend BarMediaSpectrum · Ad Intel
Highcharts
AD27
Spending Activity Search
Advertising
Granular search for specific advertiser spend occurrences by brand, network, date, creative, and daypart. Occurrence-level drill-down into when and where a specific brand ran ads. Supports ad sales prospecting and competitive intelligence at the unit level.
Occurrence Grid + FilterMediaSpectrum · Monitor-Plus
PQGrid
RIGHTS & ROYALTIES Family · 20 (RR-001–RR-020) VERIFIED — SNAP/Lionsgate · AMC RightsLogic · Rogers CRMS · BSkyB · MLB
RR-001
Avails Report — Linear/Broadcast
Rights & Royalties
Core rights availability: title × territory × rights × exclusivity × dates × runs remaining. Domestic, International, and Editable local-save variants.
Multi-Column GridSNAP · SAP IPM
PQGrid
RR-002
Monthly Available Films
Rights & Royalties
Titles available for scheduling in current month with already-utilized run counts per window.
Data GridUCI/UniMas · RightsLogic
PQGrid
RR-003
Films Expiring Report
Rights & Royalties
Active exhibition windows approaching expiration with unused runs — content at risk of being lost from schedule.
Ranked Grid + Delta BadgesUCI · AMC · Rogers
PQGrid
RR-004
Contract / Deal Status Report
Rights & Royalties
High-level deal/contract status by vendor/distributor/profit center. Active, expiring, renewed, terminated states.
Status Data GridUCI · AMC · Rogers
PQGrid
RR-005
Usage Summary / Title Usage Report
Rights & Royalties
All titles aired in period for distributor — run counts, last air date, exhibition vs. free play, total programming hours.
Data Grid + Summary BarUCI · Star
Highcharts
RR-006
Usage Letter / Licensor Report (External)
Rights & Royalties
External-facing report sent to content providers — titles aired this month + historical usage vs contracted runs. Critical compliance artifact.
Formatted Report TemplateUCI/UniMas Betty Report
PQGrid
RR-007
Runs Remaining Report
Rights & Royalties
Contracted runs remaining per asset with cost per run. Active or expired. Exhibition Window variant available.
Ranked Grid + Cost ColumnAMC RightsLogic
PQGrid
RR-008
Inventory Report (Asset Inventory)
Rights & Royalties
Full validated/published asset inventory — media rights, distributor, territory, network, max air date. Master content ledger.
Multi-Filter Data GridAMC · Star · BSkyB
PQGrid
RR-009
Media Rights Report (Territory & Rights)
Rights & Royalties
Cleared media rights by territory and asset type. Rights matrix: what is held in which markets for which content formats.
Rights Matrix GridAMC · Rogers
PQGrid
RR-010
Exhibition Restriction Report
Rights & Royalties
Restrictions against exhibition windows — conditional airing, blackout periods, authentication requirements, on-demand restrictions.
Data Grid + Flag ColumnAMC RightsLogic
PQGrid
RR-011
Deal Obligations / VOD Obligations
Rights & Royalties
Contractual delivery obligations tracking — content required vs. actual deliveries each month. Marketing/promotion obligations variant.
Progress Bar + GridRogers CRMS
Highcharts
RR-012
Royalty & Payments Report
Rights & Royalties
Deal-level royalty terms, posting history, guarantee tracking. What is owed, what has been posted, what is missing.
Stacked Bar + Data TableMLB RightsLogic
Highcharts
RR-013
Sales History Report
Rights & Royalties
Historical sales transaction ledger for Rights Out — what was sold, to whom, which territory, which period, at what fee.
Ranked Grid + FiltersSNAP SHR · MLB
PQGrid
RR-014
Graphical Sequential (Rights Gantt)
Rights & Royalties
Gantt-style: each title one row, color-coded bars for sold windows (by licensee) and available windows (green). Dashed = placeholder. Drag-and-drop interactive.
Rights Gantt (CT-NEW-07)SNAP GSR · Lionsgate
Highcharts + React
RR-015
Title Quick Check
Rights & Royalties
Single-title rapid avails check for live buyer calls. Available/sold status with full rights/territory/exclusivity in seconds.
Quick Lookup PanelSNAP TQC
React
RR-016
Avails Customer Package (Seller Report)
Rights & Royalties
Customer-facing formatted avails with Key Art, title metadata, 1/2/4-page templates per title. Exportable PDF/Excel/Word.
Formatted Report + Key ArtSNAP CRP · Lionsgate
React + PDF
RR-017
Rights Alerts & Notifications
Rights & Royalties
System-generated alerts: acquisition expirations, output notifications, TV/theatrical premiere alerts, customer-expiring title alerts.
Alert Feed + Data GridSNAP ALRT
PQGrid
RR-018
Regulatory Compliance Report (Linear & VOD)
Rights & Royalties
Master lists of linear services and VOD titles, Canadian Content ratio compliance, closed captioning. CRTC-equivalent reporting.
Compliance Grid + KPIsRogers CRMS
Highcharts
RR-019
Deal Financial Terms Export
Rights & Royalties
Full financial deal terms — rights expressions, rate cards, revenue share calculations, guarantees, conditional financial terms.
Structured Export GridRogers Finance Module
PQGrid
RR-020
Deal Profitability Analysis
Rights & Royalties
Deal costs vs viewership/subscription performance. Views Per Dollar, Views Per Subscriber, modified revenue share scenarios.
Scatter + Scenario TableRogers Conceptual
dimple.js

Total Video Consumption Report
Cross Platform
Total hours consumed across all platforms (Live+Linear, DVR, VOD, OTT/Streaming, Digital/Mobile) in single view with YoY comparison.

Highcharts
CP-003
Platform Viewing Share Report
Cross Platform
Share of total video consumption by platform type. Trend showing streaming growth vs. linear erosion.
Stacked Area + DonutNielsen TAR
Highcharts
CP-004
Weekly Reach by Platform & Demo
Cross Platform
Weekly reach % for each viewing platform by race/ethnicity with YoY delta badges. Donut badge grid visualization.
Donut Badge Grid (CT-NEW-01)Nielsen TAR Q1 2020

Platform × Network KPI Summary Card

Side-panel KPI callout with show image/logo thumbnail and single hero metric. Pull-quote companion to data tables.

React
CP-006
Platform Index Table
Cross Platform
Programs × platforms index table. Highest value per column conditionally highlighted. Performance vs. platform average index.
Index Ranking Table (CT-NEW-04)SymphonyAM VideoPulse

CP-007
Beyond L+7 Expansion Report
Cross Platform
L+7 audience vs 30-day complete run per program. Shows audience growth from extended OTT/VOD measurement window.
Paired Expansion Bar (CT-NEW-05)SymphonyAM Monetization
Highcharts
CP-008
Audience Viewing Source Venn
Cross Platform
Overlap of viewers across Live Only, DVR Only, OTT/VOD Only, and all combination segments for audience deduplication.
3-Circle Venn (CT-NEW-03)SymphonyAM CIMM
Highcharts Venn
CP-009
Streaming Brand Distribution
Cross Platform
SVOD platform brand share of total streaming consumption. Pie/treemap by service (Netflix/Hulu/PVOD/Prime/etc).
Pie / TreemapSymphonyAM
Highcharts
CP-010
Streaming Share by Age Group (YoY)
Cross Platform
Share of total streaming minutes by age cohort (P2-11, P12-17, P18-24, P25-34, P35-54, P55+) with YoY comparison.
Segmented Horiz. Bar (Paired YoY)Nielsen TAR
Highcharts
CP-011
Media Time by Demographic
Cross Platform
Daily media time (HH:MM) by race/ethnicity across all platforms. Lifestyle donut with icon center.
Lifestyle Donut (CT-NEW-22)Nielsen TAR
Highcharts
CP-012
Cross Platform Genre Consumption Trends
Cross Platform
Genre consumption bubbles over time. X=weeks, Y=total minutes viewed, bubble size=# episodes. Genre as color.
Bubble ScatterVMN CPR / Viacom
dimple.js
CP-ADV-001
Media Plan Gender/Demo Skew
Cross Platform
Gender and demographic skew index for a proposed media plan against a target audience. Diverging bar from 100-base.
Diverging BarPlan-it / Viacom
Highcharts
CP-ADV-002
CP TRP Delivery Scorecard
Cross Platform
Cross-platform TRP delivery: Linear/Digital/Social/OTT. Estimate vs. Actual vs. % Diff. 2-panel paired scorecard grid.
Paired Scorecard (CT-ADV-03)Plan-it / Viacom
Highcharts

Digital Analytics

Ranked Report (Top N)
Digital Analytics
Top N items for any metric sorted by value. Pages, Site Sections, Products, Videos by visits/views/revenue.
Horizontal Bar + GridAdobe Analytics
Highcharts + PQGrid
DA-003
Trended Report (Metric Over Time)
Digital Analytics
Any metric tracked across time periods for up to 5 items simultaneously. Trend Line, Smooth Line (video), Area, Stacked Area variants.
Trend / Smooth / AreaAdobe Analytics
Highcharts
DA-004
Conversion Funnel Report
Digital Analytics
% of visitors progressing through defined event sequence (Visit → Cart → Checkout → Purchase). Fallout rate at each step.
Funnel VisualizationAdobe Analytics
Highcharts
DA-005
Fallout Report (Path Analysis)
Digital Analytics
Visitors through pre-specified page sequence. Conversion AND fallout rates between each checkpoint. Non-linear branching paths supported.
Stepped Funnel w/ BranchAdobe Analytics
Highcharts
DA-006
Page Flow / Next Page Report
Digital Analytics
Tree-based visualization of visitor page order from a selected page. Line widths proportional to traffic share. 3 levels deep. Forward or backward variants.
Sankey Navigation (CT-NEW-08)Adobe Analytics
D3
DA-007
Marketing Channel Report
Digital Analytics
Which external channels drive traffic and conversion. First Touch vs. Last Touch attribution. Paid/Organic/Social/Display/Email/Direct split.
Stacked Bar + Ranked TableAdobe Analytics
Highcharts
DA-008
Visitor Retention Report
Digital Analytics
Weekly cohort-based visitor return rate. Day 1/7/14/30 retention curves. New vs. returning visitor composition over time.
Cohort Heatmap + LineAdobe Analytics
Highcharts
DA-009
Visitor Profile (Geographic)
Digital Analytics
Visitor country/state/city distribution. DMA choropleth for US-focused sites. Language + locale breakdown. Mobile vs. desktop by geography.
Choropleth Map + Ranked BarAdobe Analytics
D3 + topojson
DA-010
Video Engagement / Smooth Line Report
Digital Analytics
% of views for each video time segment. Dropout curve. Rises indicate replay/rewind. Peaks identify most-rewatched moments.
Smooth Line Engagement (CT-NEW-09)Adobe Analytics Video
Highcharts
DA-011
Products / Conversion Report
Digital Analytics
Product-level revenue, units, conversion rate, average order value. Category hierarchy with drill-down. Subscription vs. transactional splits.
Ranked Bar + TreemapAdobe Analytics
Highcharts
DA-012
Custom Traffic Report (Prop)
Digital Analytics
Custom traffic reporting using Omniture/Adobe Traffic Properties (Props). Counter, list, and hierarchical prop types. Pathing enabled.
Ranked Grid + TrendAdobe Analytics (Omniture)
PQGrid
DA-013
Custom Conversion Report (eVar)
Digital Analytics
Custom conversion reporting using eVar variables. Linear, participation, and last-touch allocation. Campaign-level conversion attribution.
Ranked Grid + FunnelAdobe Analytics (Omniture)
PQGrid
DA-014
Segmented / Breakdown Report
Digital Analytics
Any report broken down by a second dimension. Traffic × Traffic (correlations) or Conversion × Conversion (subrelations). Up to 20 levels deep. 3-Metric Bubble variant.
Nested Grid + Bubble (CT-NEW-16)Adobe Analytics
PQGrid
OTHER / MEDIA INTELLIGENCE Family · 8 (OT-001–OT-008) SOURCE — Evan Shapiro / ESHAP · Media War & Peace (eshap.substack.com) · Media Universe Maps 2020–2026 · Webby Award 2026
Evan Shapiro — Media Cartographer & ESHAP Founder · Emmy & Peabody Award-winning producer (Portlandia, Comedy Bang Bang, Brick City, Please Like Me). Former President, IFC Channel. Professor, NYU Stern & Fordham. 27,000+ Substack subscribers. Media Universe Map redrawn monthly by hand since August 2020 from public data only. 2026 Six Themes: ① YouTube=TV ② Affinity Economy ③ Creator Economy=Half the Map ④ Sports as Anchor ⑤ AI & Measurement ⑥ Regulatory Reckoning for Big Tech. YouTube confirmed #1 US TV channel every month since April 2025.
OT-001
Media Universe Map Report
Other / Media Intelligence
Market-cap-scaled solar system of ~125 entities (Big Tech, legacy media, gaming, audio, social, video) redrawn monthly from public data. 2025 restructure adds Creator Economy, AI, Measurement, Global platforms, PSM. Feb 2026: first version where Alphabet > Apple.
Scaled Bubble Force MapESHAP / Public Financials
D3 Force
OT-002
Quarterly Earnings Intelligence Report
Other / Media Intelligence
Earnings call transcript vs actual financial numbers delta for 100+ publicly traded media companies per quarter. Covers Versant, Canal+, ITV, Netflix, Disney, WBD, Paramount, Fox. Tracks trajectory shifts before they appear in market prices.
Multi-Line + Delta TableESHAP / SEC Filings / Earnings Calls
Highcharts + PQGrid
OT-003
Attention Share Report (Cross-Screen Index)
Other / Media Intelligence
The ESHAP Cross-Screen Attention Index — first measurement of the whole viewer across all screens simultaneously. Published 'against great resistance from Delta Airlines.' Minutes of attention per entity, per platform, per demographic cohort.
Stacked Bar + Indexed ComparisonESHAP Cross-Screen Study
Highcharts
OT-004
Streaming Platform Landscape Analysis
Other / Media Intelligence
SVOD/AVOD/FAST landscape: subscriber counts, viewership share, revenue trajectory, content spend, ad-supported tier penetration, bundling, and consolidation. ESHAP YouTube Landscape Analysis (Summer 2025) as standalone sub-report.
Ranked Bar + Trend Lines + PieESHAP / Deloitte / Nielsen
Highcharts
OT-005
YouTube on TV Intelligence Report
Other / Media Intelligence
YouTube CTV viewership vs. all traditional TV entities — monthly rank tracking since April 2025 (#1 US TV). Beats all Disney channels combined, all NBCU, Paramount+CBS, Fox+Fox News. Excludes YouTube TV, mobile, desktop, Shorts.
Ranked Bar + Multi-Line TrendESHAP / Nielsen / YouTube Data
Highcharts
OT-006
Creator Economy Market Report
Other / Media Intelligence
Creator economy scale vs. legacy media: revenue, audience, valuation, growth. 'Yindie Film' case studies. 2026 'Worlds Collide' thesis: Trad Media + Creators unite. Validates 'half the map' projection: Netflix now worth more than Comcast + Disney + Paramount + Fox + WBD combined.
Bubble Map + Growth TrendESHAP / Platform Earnings
D3 + Highcharts
OT-007
Media's Generation Gap Report
Other / Media Intelligence
ESHAP standalone report (Summer 2025): viewing habits, platform preferences, attention duration, and content format affinity by Gen Z / Millennial / Gen X / Boomer. Validates 'younger consumers pay more than any other cohort when content earns their affinity.'
Grouped Bar + Donut by GenerationESHAP Media's Generation Gap (2025)
Highcharts
OT-008
Cord-Cutter / Streaming Migration Tracker
Other / Media Intelligence
Pay TV household erosion model vs. streaming net subscriber adds. AVOD tier growth. 'Winner-take-most' consolidation trajectory. SVOD price hike impact on churn vs. ad-tier adoption. Amazon as 'new cable company' (Shapiro 2025 thesis).
Dual-Line Diverging + AreaESHAP / AlixPartners / Deloitte
Highcharts
05

Brand Characters

SaSMaster's visual identity is anchored by two distinct mascots that personify the platform's core values: intelligence, energy, and accessibility. These characters appear across touchpoints—from the portal floating FAB to marketing materials and user onboarding.

Dr. Scoop

The intelligent analyst. Dr. Scoop is SaSMaster's floating AI assistant—a friendly, always-on guide with a screen for a mind. Represents deep insight, real-time intelligence, and a touch of personality.

Personality Traits Data-driven · Responsive · Approachable · Always observing

Usage Portal FAB · Contextual help overlays · Onboarding tours · Email signatures

Frankenstein Media Monkey

The energetic tinkerer. Built from media industry tools and powered by pure curiosity, this character embodies creativity, experimentation, and the art of connecting disparate data sources into coherent intelligence.

Personality Traits Experimental · Energetic · Resourceful · Playfully complex

Usage Marketing materials · Campaign hero art · Case study headers · Social content

Character Usage Guidelines

✓ Do
  • Use Dr. Scoop for in-app guidance and support
  • Feature Frankenstein in marketing & external comms
  • Maintain the characters' distinct visual identity
  • Scale characters proportionally, never distort
  • Ensure adequate whitespace around characters
✗ Don't
  • Combine both characters in the same composition
  • Use as background pattern or texture
  • Apply heavy filters or color overlays
  • Rotate or flip the characters
  • Use in contexts inconsistent with their personality

Character Deployment Matrix

TouchpointPrimary CharacterContextFormat
Portal FABDr. ScoopAlways-visible contextual assistant, pulsing at restVector SVG, 64×64px
Onboarding ModalDr. ScoopStep-by-step guidance, feature orientationIllustration, 160×200px
Error StatesDr. ScoopEncouraging tone, "I'm here to help"Icon variant, 48×48px
Marketing HeroFrankenstein Media MonkeyCampaign splash, energetic brand momentArtwork, 1200×800px+
Case Study HeadersFrankenstein Media MonkeyVisual break, success narrativeIllustration, 600×400px
Social ContentFrankenstein Media MonkeyShareability, personality, thought leadershipSquare 1:1, variable sizes
Email SignatureDr. ScoopSubtle brand reinforcementIcon, 24×24px
Blog/Article BylineFrankenstein Media MonkeyExperimental insights, deep research piecesAvatar, 80×80px
06

Motion System & Elevation

Motion is state-change only in the dark theme. No idle animation except Dr. Scoop pulse and the metric strip scan line.

TokenDurationEasingUse Case
hover150mscubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)Card hover, button hover, color transitions
press200mscubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)Button press, badge change
panel300mscubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)Panel slide-in/out, dropdown open, tooltip
page400mscubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)Page section transitions, modal enter
data-reveal600ms + stagger(50ms)cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1)Card grid load, chart bar grow
scan-line1200ms looplinearMetric strip data load sweep
ElevationCSSUse Case
E1 — card0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)Cards floating on --bg-base
E2 — panel0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.6), 0 0 0 1px --borderPanels, sidebars above content
E3 — modal0 8px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.8), 0 0 0 1px --border-activeModals, dropdowns, tooltips
lav-glow0 0 16px rgba(167,139,250,0.2)Active/selected card states
teal-glow0 0 12px rgba(34,211,238,0.3)Live/streaming indicators
